---
title: "Water Sampling Status Report"
author: "Author: A.J. Brown, Agricultural Data Scientist"
date: "`r format(Sys.Date(), '%B %d, %Y')`"
knit: (function(input_file, encoding) {
    rmarkdown::render(
      input_file,
      encoding = encoding,
      output_dir = file.path(dirname(input_file), "..", "YTD Water Report")
    )
  })
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 1
    toc_float:
      collapsed: true
      smooth_scroll: true
    code_folding: hide
    df_print: paged
---

<div style="text-align:center; margin: 8px auto 24px auto;">
  <a href="https://agsci.colostate.edu/waterquality/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">
    <img src="https://agsci.colostate.edu/waterquality/wp-content/uploads/sites/160/2024/05/AWQP_horizontalhighres-1280x435.png"
         alt="Colorado State University Agriculture Water Quality Program"
         style="width: min(100%, 720px); height: auto;">
  </a>
</div>

```{css, echo=FALSE}
.main-container { max-width: 96% !important; width: 96% !important; }
.tocify { width: 250px; }
.status-grid { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(165px, 1fr)); gap: 12px; margin: 18px 0; }
.status-card { border: 1px solid #d8dee4; border-radius: 6px; padding: 12px 16px; background: #f8fafc; }
.status-value { font-size: 1.8em; font-weight: 600; color: #1f5d42; }
.status-label { color: #4b5563; }
.dataTables_wrapper { overflow-x: auto; }
.tab-content { padding-top: 16px; }
.site-selector-callout {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  flex-wrap: wrap;
  gap: 14px;
  padding: 18px 20px;
  margin: 14px 0 20px 0;
  border: 2px solid #1f6f4a;
  border-radius: 8px;
  background: #eef8f2;
}
.site-selector-callout .selector-label {
  margin: 0;
  font-size: 1.1em;
  font-weight: 600;
  color: #174c35;
}
.site-selector-callout .btn {
  min-width: 250px;
  font-weight: 600;
  box-shadow: 0 2px 5px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.18);
}
.site-selector-callout .dropdown-menu {
  max-height: 360px;
  overflow-y: auto;
  min-width: 320px;
}
.site-plot-container {
  min-height: 740px;
  margin-top: 20px;
  margin-bottom: 36px;
  clear: both;
}
.site-plot-container .plotly.html-widget {
  height: 700px !important;
}
```

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE,
                      fig.width = 11, fig.height = 6)
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = here::here())

package.list <- c("dplyr", "tidyr", "stringr", "DT", "plotly", "here", "htmltools")
packageLoad <- function(packages) {
  for (package in packages) {
    if (!require(package, character.only = TRUE)) {
      install.packages(package, repos = "https://cloud.r-project.org")
      library(package, character.only = TRUE)
    }
  }
}
packageLoad(package.list)

setwd(here::here())
source(here::here("Code", "file-merger.R"), local = knitr::knit_global())
dat <- returnAllFiles(d = directory, export = TRUE)
names(dat) <- tolower(names(dat))

required_columns <- c("sample.id", "analyte", "result", "units", "collected",
                      "location.name", "treatment.name", "event.type")
missing_columns <- setdiff(required_columns, names(dat))
if (length(missing_columns) > 0) {
  stop("Water report is missing required columns: ", paste(missing_columns, collapse = ", "))
}

dat <- dat %>%
  mutate(
    across(c(sample.id, analyte, units, location.name, treatment.name, event.type),
           ~ na_if(stringr::str_squish(as.character(.x)), "")),
    collected_date = as.Date(collected),
    is_duplicate = stringr::str_detect(sample.id, "-D$"),
    primary_sample_id = stringr::str_remove(sample.id, "-D$")
  )

sample_inventory <- dat %>%
  distinct(sample.id, primary_sample_id, is_duplicate, location.name,
           treatment.name, event.type, collected_date)

pair_status <- sample_inventory %>%
  filter(!is.na(primary_sample_id)) %>%
  group_by(primary_sample_id) %>%
  summarise(
    has_primary = any(!is_duplicate),
    has_duplicate = any(is_duplicate),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

report_years <- sort(unique(format(dat$collected_date[!is.na(dat$collected_date)], "%Y")))
priority_year <- max(as.integer(report_years), na.rm = TRUE)
date_min <- suppressWarnings(min(dat$collected_date, na.rm = TRUE))
date_max <- suppressWarnings(max(dat$collected_date, na.rm = TRUE))

dt_options <- list(
  pageLength = 15,
  scrollX = TRUE,
  autoWidth = TRUE,
  dom = "Bfrtip",
  buttons = c("copy", "csv", "excel")
)

site_plot <- function(site_data) {
  plot_data <- site_data %>%
    filter(!is.na(analyte), !is.na(result), !is.na(collected_date)) %>%
    mutate(treatment_plot = coalesce(treatment.name, "[Missing treatment]"))

  analytes <- sort(unique(plot_data$analyte))
  if (length(analytes) == 0) return(htmltools::p("No plottable results for this site."))

  treatments <- sort(unique(plot_data$treatment_plot))
  treatment_colors <- setNames(grDevices::hcl.colors(length(treatments), "Dark 3"), treatments)
  p <- plotly::plot_ly(height = 700)
  trace_analytes <- character(0)

  for (analyte_value in analytes) {
    analyte_data <- plot_data %>% filter(analyte == analyte_value)
    for (treatment_value in sort(unique(analyte_data$treatment_plot))) {
      trace_data <- analyte_data %>% filter(treatment_plot == treatment_value)
      p <- plotly::add_markers(
        p, data = trace_data,
        x = ~collected_date, y = ~result,
        name = treatment_value,
        legendgroup = treatment_value,
        marker = list(color = treatment_colors[[treatment_value]], size = 8),
        text = ~paste0(
          "Sample ID: ", sample.id,
          "<br>Treatment: ", treatment_plot,
          "<br>Event type: ", coalesce(event.type, "[Missing]"),
          "<br>Date: ", collected_date,
          "<br>Result: ", result, " ", coalesce(units, "")
        ),
        hoverinfo = "text",
        visible = identical(analyte_value, analytes[[1]])
      )
      trace_analytes <- c(trace_analytes, analyte_value)
    }
  }

  units_by_analyte <- vapply(analytes, function(a) {
    values <- sort(unique(na.omit(plot_data$units[plot_data$analyte == a])))
    if (length(values) == 0) "" else paste(values, collapse = ", ")
  }, character(1))

  buttons <- lapply(seq_along(analytes), function(i) {
    analyte_value <- analytes[[i]]
    y_title <- if (nzchar(units_by_analyte[[i]])) {
      paste0("Result (", units_by_analyte[[i]], ")")
    } else {
      "Result"
    }
    list(
      method = "update",
      args = list(
        list(visible = trace_analytes == analyte_value),
        list(title = analyte_value, yaxis = list(title = y_title))
      ),
      label = analyte_value
    )
  })

  initial_y_title <- if (nzchar(units_by_analyte[[1]])) {
    paste0("Result (", units_by_analyte[[1]], ")")
  } else {
    "Result"
  }

  plotly::layout(
    p,
    title = analytes[[1]],
    xaxis = list(title = "Collection date"),
    yaxis = list(title = initial_y_title),
    hovermode = "closest",
    legend = list(orientation = "h", y = -0.2),
    margin = list(t = 100, b = 110),
    updatemenus = list(list(
      type = "dropdown", buttons = buttons,
      x = 0, xanchor = "left", y = 1.16, yanchor = "top",
      direction = "down", showactive = TRUE
    ))
  )
}
```

# Overview

This report covers collection dates from `r format(date_min, '%Y-%m-%d')` through
`r format(date_max, '%Y-%m-%d')`. Counts are calculated from distinct sample IDs,
not analyte result rows. A duplicate is an ID ending in `-D`; its primary ID is
the same ID with only that terminal suffix removed.

```{r overview-cards, results='asis'}
overview <- list(
  "Sites" = n_distinct(na.omit(sample_inventory$location.name)),
  "Collection events" = n_distinct(na.omit(sample_inventory$collected_date)),
  "All sample IDs" = n_distinct(na.omit(sample_inventory$sample.id)),
  "Primary sample IDs" = n_distinct(na.omit(sample_inventory$sample.id[!sample_inventory$is_duplicate])),
  "Duplicate IDs" = n_distinct(na.omit(sample_inventory$sample.id[sample_inventory$is_duplicate])),
  "Matched duplicate pairs" = sum(pair_status$has_primary & pair_status$has_duplicate)
)
cat('<div class="status-grid">')
for (label in names(overview)) {
  cat(sprintf('<div class="status-card"><div class="status-value">%s</div><div class="status-label">%s</div></div>',
              format(overview[[label]], big.mark = ","), label))
}
cat('</div>')
```

Primary IDs without a matched duplicate: **`r sum(pair_status$has_primary & !pair_status$has_duplicate)`**.
This is informational; a duplicate is not expected for every primary sample.

# Sampling priorities

The AWQP growing-season target is at least **three sampling events per
location–treatment pair**. The table below highlights observed pairs that have
not yet reached that target in **`r priority_year`**. An event is one distinct
collection date, so duplicate bottles and multiple analyte results do not
inflate this count.

<div class="alert alert-warning" role="alert">
<strong>Before scheduling additional field collection:</strong> Check ongoing
analysis in the <a href="https://webtrieveus.alsenviro.com/Login.aspx"
target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">ALS Webtrieve portal</a>. Recently
submitted samples may still be under analysis and therefore absent from this
report's completed ALS results.
</div>

```{r sampling-priorities}
sampling_target <- 3L

priority_table <- sample_inventory %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(collected_date),
    as.integer(format(collected_date, "%Y")) == priority_year,
    !is.na(location.name),
    !is.na(treatment.name),
    !location.name %in% c("Lab Blank", "Method Blank", "Lab Control Sample"),
    !event.type %in% c("Lab Blank", "Method Blank", "Lab Control Sample")
  ) %>%
  group_by(location.name, treatment.name) %>%
  summarise(
    samples_collected = n_distinct(collected_date),
    samples_still_needed = pmax(sampling_target - samples_collected, 0L),
    collection_dates = paste(
      sort(unique(format(collected_date, "%Y-%m-%d"))),
      collapse = ", "
    ),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(samples_collected < sampling_target) %>%
  arrange(desc(samples_still_needed), location.name, treatment.name)

if (nrow(priority_table) == 0) {
  htmltools::div(
    class = "alert alert-success",
    paste("All observed location–treatment pairs have at least", sampling_target,
          "sampling events in", priority_year, ".")
  )
} else {
  DT::datatable(
    priority_table,
    rownames = FALSE,
    filter = "top",
    caption = paste(
      "Location–treatment pairs below the",
      sampling_target,
      "event target for",
      priority_year
    ),
    extensions = "Buttons",
    options = modifyList(
      dt_options,
      list(
        pageLength = 25,
        order = list(list(2, "desc"), list(0, "asc")),
        columnDefs = list(list(className = "dt-center", targets = c(2, 3)))
      )
    )
  ) %>%
    DT::formatStyle(
      "samples_still_needed",
      backgroundColor = DT::styleInterval(c(1, 2), c("#fff3cd", "#f8d7da", "#dc3545")),
      color = DT::styleInterval(2, c("#212529", "#ffffff")),
      fontWeight = "bold"
    )
}
```

This table can only evaluate location–treatment pairs represented by at least
one sample in the current report. Planned pairs with **zero** samples require a
separate sampling-plan reference and will not appear here.

# Sampling coverage

An event is a distinct collection date for a location/treatment combination.
The bottle columns separately show distinct primary and duplicate sample IDs.

```{r coverage-table}
coverage <- sample_inventory %>%
  mutate(
    location.name = coalesce(location.name, "[Missing location]"),
    treatment.name = coalesce(treatment.name, "[Missing treatment]")
  ) %>%
  group_by(location.name, treatment.name) %>%
  summarise(
    events = n_distinct(collected_date, na.rm = TRUE),
    primary_sample_ids = n_distinct(sample.id[!is_duplicate], na.rm = TRUE),
    duplicate_ids = n_distinct(sample.id[is_duplicate], na.rm = TRUE),
    collection_dates = paste(sort(unique(format(collected_date[!is.na(collected_date)], "%Y-%m-%d"))), collapse = ", "),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(location.name, treatment.name)

DT::datatable(
  coverage,
  rownames = FALSE,
  filter = "top",
  caption = "Sampling coverage by location and treatment",
  extensions = "Buttons",
  options = dt_options
)
```

# Explore WQ data

Use the site dropdown to show its treatment/event summary and results. Within
each plot, use the analyte dropdown above the graph. Points are colored by
treatment.

```{r site-panels}
sites <- sort(unique(na.omit(dat$location.name)))
site_menu <- vector("list", length(sites))
site_panels <- vector("list", length(sites))

for (i in seq_along(sites)) {
  site <- sites[[i]]
  site_data <- dat %>% filter(location.name == site)
  site_coverage <- coverage %>% filter(location.name == site) %>% select(-location.name)
  panel_id <- paste0("water-site-", i)

  site_menu[[i]] <- htmltools::tags$li(
    htmltools::tags$a(href = paste0("#", panel_id), `data-toggle` = "tab", site)
  )
  site_panels[[i]] <- htmltools::tags$div(
    id = panel_id,
    class = paste("tab-pane", if (i == 1) "active" else ""),
    htmltools::tags$h3(site),
    DT::datatable(
    site_coverage,
    rownames = FALSE,
    caption = paste("Sampling status:", site),
    options = list(dom = "t", scrollX = TRUE)
    ),
    htmltools::tags$div(
      class = "site-plot-container",
      site_plot(site_data)
    )
  )
}

htmltools::tagList(
  htmltools::tags$div(
    class = "site-selector-callout",
    htmltools::tags$p(class = "selector-label", "Select a site to view its sampling status and water-quality results:"),
    htmltools::tags$div(
      class = "dropdown",
      htmltools::tags$button(
        class = "btn btn-success btn-lg dropdown-toggle",
        type = "button", `data-toggle` = "dropdown", `aria-haspopup` = "true",
        `aria-expanded` = "false",
        "Choose a site to explore ", htmltools::tags$span(class = "caret")
      ),
      htmltools::tags$ul(class = "dropdown-menu", site_menu)
    )
  ),
  htmltools::tags$div(class = "tab-content", site_panels)
)
```

# Analytes present

```{r analyte-summary}
analyte_summary <- dat %>%
  filter(!is.na(analyte)) %>%
  group_by(analyte) %>%
  summarise(
    methods = paste(sort(unique(na.omit(method))), collapse = "; "),
    sample_ids = n_distinct(sample.id),
    primary_sample_ids = n_distinct(sample.id[!is_duplicate]),
    result_rows = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(analyte)

DT::datatable(
  analyte_summary,
  rownames = FALSE,
  filter = "top",
  caption = "Analytes, methods, and overall counts",
  extensions = "Buttons",
  options = dt_options
)
```

# Quality control

The QC table is sample-level and flags missing or unmapped fields needed by this
report. Use the CSV or Excel button to export the currently filtered error report.

```{r qc-report}
qc_source <- dat %>%
  distinct(sample.id, location.name, treatment.name, event.type, collected_date,
           analyte, method)

qc_report <- bind_rows(
  qc_source %>% filter(is.na(sample.id)) %>% mutate(issue = "Missing sample ID"),
  qc_source %>% filter(is.na(location.name)) %>% mutate(issue = "Missing/unmapped location"),
  qc_source %>% filter(is.na(treatment.name)) %>% mutate(issue = "Missing/unmapped treatment"),
  qc_source %>% filter(is.na(collected_date)) %>% mutate(issue = "Missing/unparseable collection date"),
  qc_source %>% filter(is.na(analyte)) %>% mutate(issue = "Missing analyte"),
  qc_source %>% filter(is.na(method)) %>% mutate(issue = "Missing method")
) %>%
  group_by(sample.id, location.name, treatment.name, event.type, collected_date, analyte) %>%
  summarise(issue = paste(sort(unique(issue)), collapse = "; "), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(issue, sample.id)

if (nrow(qc_report) == 0) {
  htmltools::div(class = "alert alert-success", "No missing or unmapped required fields were found.")
} else {
  DT::datatable(
    qc_report,
    rownames = FALSE,
    filter = "top",
    caption = paste(nrow(qc_report), "sample/analyte records require review"),
    extensions = "Buttons",
    options = dt_options
  )
}
```

# Raw data

```{r raw-data}
DT::datatable(
  dat,
  rownames = FALSE,
  filter = "top",
  extensions = "Buttons",
  options = modifyList(dt_options, list(pageLength = 25))
)
```
